methodsAttempted.query: site:auburn.edu Auburn University generative AI policy guidance students faculty artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Auburn OACP, Graduate School, Biggio Center, Provost, Academic Integrity, and Libraries pages.
Robots.txt for auburn.edu and www.auburn.edu did not disallow the selected policy and guidance paths.
A Libraries link to the IT AI tools KB resolved to a login-required ServiceNow article and was rejected as inaccessible.

artifacts.summary: Discovery verified five official Auburn sources with usable AI policy or guidance text, rejected one duplicate pointer page, and marked one official IT KB page login-required.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Data defined as "operational data" or "confidential data" in the Auburn University Data Classification Policy or the AUM Data Classification Policy should never be shared with, submitted to, or used with artificial intelligence (AI) tools or systems in the absence of specific, legally binding data security protection agreements and procedures.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Instructional faculty may incorporate AI tools into their teaching methods and assessments but should clearly communicate the use and role of AI tools to students. Faculty have freedom to encourage or restrict student use of AI tools in their courses.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Usage Guidelines for Graduate Students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Usage of AI by graduate students in research and writing must be disclosed and approved by the student’s advisory committee. The disclosure will include the various ways AI tools have been used in conducting the research and/or writing the thesis, or dissertation, or other academic document.

artifacts.notes: Claim is narrowly tied to OACP responsible AI guidance and preserves the agreement/procedure condition.
Claim avoids university-wide student prohibition and follows the course-level faculty discretion language.
Claim is limited to the student guidance page and does not infer an all-course policy.
Claim preserves the source caveat that these are optional guidelines and not official university policy.
Claim is scoped to graduate-student research and writing guidance.
Claim uses the public Libraries guide only and does not quote inaccessible ServiceNow KB text.

artifacts.title: Auburn University AI Policy Evidence Staging Summary
artifacts.summary: Staged Auburn University AI policy and guidance candidates from official OACP, Provost, Graduate School, and Libraries sources. Claims cover responsible AI data handling, course-level instructional expectations, student academic-integrity guidance, optional course AI-use templates, graduate disclosure expectations, and affiliated AI resources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Provost course AI-use page is explicitly optional and not official university policy.
The linked Auburn ServiceNow AI tools article required login and was not used as direct claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
